name: satellite_imagery_indigenous_site
tension_description: Scientific disclosure versus cultural stewardship of sacred knowledge.
premise: Remote-sensing archaeologist Dr. Mateo Silva has identified what appears to be an extensive precolonial ceremonial complex beneath rainforest canopy using high-resolution satellite imagery funded by a university lab. The discovery could transform regional history and strengthen legal arguments against mining concessions already being negotiated nearby. However, the Indigenous council whose oral traditions point to the same area says publicizing the location would invite looting, tourism pressure, and yet another academic scramble to claim knowledge the community has safeguarded for centuries. A major journal has offered fast-track publication if Mateo releases the coordinates and methodology immediately, and the national ministry is urging publicity to block the mining deal before contracts are signed. Mateo can either publish now with location data or delay publication until the council helps design a restricted disclosure plan.

agents.name: Dr. Mateo Silva
agents.identity: I am an archaeologist committed to using science to protect endangered histories before industry erases them. I also know my field has a long record of treating Indigenous knowledge as a resource to extract rather than a relationship to honor.
agents.goal: I want to stop the mining concession and contribute to historical truth without exposing a sacred site to new forms of exploitation. I need to decide whether urgent publication protects the site or repeats the very harm it seeks to prevent.

agents.name: Chairwoman Yara Kintu
agents.identity: I am the elected chairwoman of the council whose elders have protected the stories and boundaries of this place through decades of displacement. We have watched outsiders call our knowledge valuable only when they can publish it.
agents.goal: I want Mateo to delay public release until the council controls how the site is described and shielded, even if that slows the legal fight against mining. I believe stewardship of sacred knowledge is part of justice, not a barrier to it.

choices: I will publish the findings and coordinates immediately to mobilize international pressure against the mining concessions before the contracts are finalized.
I will delay publication until the Indigenous council and my lab create a restricted disclosure plan, even if that weakens the short-term campaign against mining.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Dr. Mateo Silva must decide whether to publish the site data immediately or wait for a council-led disclosure plan. What does Dr. Mateo Silva do?
